Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip

Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and low HbA1c. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction with protein loss and ketone presence, possibly related to poor nutritional status or catabolic state. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
